# App Review & Refactor Plan

## Context
Full architectural review of the Otodom-powered luxury real estate site. The user wants the app to update dynamically (ideally without rebuilding) when a new XML delivery arrives every ~2 hours, with fast filtering, SEO, lightweight thumbnails, dynamic agents, and dynamic blog.

---

## 1. ⚠️ Core Architecture — Static vs Dynamic (most important decision)

**Current state:** `output: 'static'` on GitHub Pages. Every offer update requires a full rebuild and redeploy. Property detail pages are generated by `getStaticPaths()` — they **must** be pre-rendered to exist at all.

**Target:** GitHub Pages for testing → own VPS for production. Design for SSR from the start.

### Recommended: Hybrid SSR with `@astrojs/node`

```js
// astro.config.mjs
import node from '@astrojs/node';
export default defineConfig({
  output: 'server',           // SSR by default
  adapter: node({ mode: 'standalone' }),
  integrations: [react(), sitemap()],
});
```

**Per-page rendering strategy:**

| Page | Strategy | Rationale |
|------|----------|-----------|
| `index.astro` | SSR (`prerender = false`) | Always serves fresh offer list from XML on disk |
| `property/[id].astro` | SSR (`prerender = false`) | Fresh on each request, no stale detail pages |
| `blog/index.astro` | Static (`prerender = true`) | Blog rarely changes |
| `blog/[...slug].astro` | Static (`prerender = true`) | Same |
| `contact.astro` | Static (`prerender = true`) | Same |

With this setup: the external process drops a new `properties_otodom.xml` on disk every 2 hours, and the next page request immediately picks it up — zero redeploy needed.

**Property detail pages are SSR too** — this means new offers appear instantly without a rebuild, and old/deactivated offers return 404 server-side rather than staying as stale HTML forever.

**GitHub Pages testing:** Keep a separate `astro.config.gh-pages.mjs` with `output: 'static'` and a scheduled Actions rebuild (`*/120 * * * *`) that runs `astro build --config astro.config.gh-pages.mjs`. This lets the same codebase serve both environments.

**SEO on SSR:** Astro SSR renders full HTML server-side on each request — crawlers get the same pre-rendered HTML they would from a static build. No SEO regression.

**Serving on VPS:**
```bash
node ./dist/server/entry.mjs   # standalone Node.js server
# or behind nginx reverse proxy on port 4321
```

---

## 2. Hardcoded XML path — must fix regardless of architecture choice

**Current:** Two files hardcode `'public/2026-05-23_13%3A07%3A04/properties_otodom.xml'` and the photo base URL with the same timestamp.
- `src/pages/index.astro:13`
- `src/pages/property/[id].astro:12`

**Fix:** Introduce a single config/env variable so the delivery process only needs to write to a stable location.

```
# .env / .env.production
OFFERS_XML_PATH=public/offers/latest/properties_otodom.xml
OFFERS_PHOTO_BASE=offers/latest/
```

Or simpler: the delivery script always writes to `public/offers/current/` (stable path, no timestamps in code).

Both pages import the path from one place: `src/utils/offersPath.js` → `export const XML_PATH` / `export const PHOTO_BASE`.

---

## 3. Image optimization — significant gap

**Current:** Every `<img>` is a raw tag. No `loading="lazy"`, no `srcset`, no WebP, no Astro `<Image>`. Property photos live in `public/` (bypasses Astro's image pipeline).

**Problem with `public/` images:** Astro's `<Image>` component can't optimize them at build time (they're not in `src/assets/`). Options:

### A — Astro Image with `inferSize` + `width` override (easiest)
```astro
import { Image } from 'astro:assets';
// For thumbnails in FeaturedProperties — 640px, WebP
<Image src={imageUrl} width={640} height={480} format="webp" quality={75} alt={city} loading="lazy" />
// For hero in property detail — full width
<Image src={imageUrl} width={1400} format="webp" quality={85} alt={city} loading="eager" />
```
Works for remote URLs (Unsplash etc.) and absolute paths. Needs `experimental.assets: true` or Astro 4+ default image service.

### B — Add `@astrojs/image` / sharp and configure a local image service
- Add `image: { service: sharpImageService() }` to `astro.config.mjs`
- Move delivery photos to `src/assets/offers/` symlinked or copied by the delivery script
- Full pipeline: resize → WebP → cache

**Minimum viable improvement (do this now):**
- Add `loading="lazy"` to all below-fold `<img>` tags in `FeaturedProperties.jsx`, `AgentGrid.jsx`, `RelatedCarousel.jsx`, blog index
- Keep `loading="eager"` only on the hero/main image of each page
- Add `decoding="async"` on all non-critical images

**Thumbnail vs full resolution:**
- Listing cards (`FeaturedProperties`): render at `width=600` WebP
- Property hero: render at `width=1400` WebP
- Gallery side images: render at `width=700` WebP

---

## 4. SEO — critical missing pieces

**Current:** Only `<title>` and `<meta name="description">` are set. No OG, no structured data, no sitemap, no robots.txt.

### 4a — Open Graph + Twitter Card in Layout.astro
Extend `Layout.astro` props to accept `ogImage`, `ogType`, `canonicalUrl`. Property detail pages pass the main property photo as `ogImage`. Blog posts pass `thumbnail`.

### 4b — JSON-LD structured data
- Property pages: `schema.org/RealEstateListing` with price, address, image, agent
- Blog posts: `schema.org/BlogPosting`
- Homepage: `schema.org/RealEstateAgent` for the agency

### 4c — Sitemap
```js
// astro.config.mjs
import sitemap from '@astrojs/sitemap';
integrations: [react(), sitemap()],
```
Add `@astrojs/sitemap` — zero config, auto-generates `/sitemap-index.xml`.

### 4d — robots.txt
Add `public/robots.txt`:
```
User-agent: *
Allow: /
Sitemap: https://pientkrz.github.io/real-estate-vendor/sitemap-index.xml
```

### 4e — Canonical URLs
Add `<link rel="canonical" href={canonicalUrl} />` in Layout.astro using `Astro.url`.

---

## 5. Agent data — move out of component

**Current:** 4 agents hardcoded in `AgentGrid.jsx` (name, role, Unsplash photo URL, phone, email, languages[]).

**Fix:** Astro content collection, same pattern as blog.

```
src/content/agents/
  monika-chwajol.md
  wojciech-jaskula.md
  krzysztof-jaruszowiec.md
  szymon-janas.md
```

Schema in `content.config.ts`:
```ts
const agents = defineCollection({
  loader: glob({ pattern: '**/*.md', base: './src/content/agents' }),
  schema: z.object({
    name: z.string(),
    role: z.string(),
    photo: z.string(),       // path to src/assets/agents/ or remote URL
    phone: z.string(),
    email: z.string(),
    languages: z.array(z.string()),
    order: z.number().optional(), // for explicit sort order
  }),
});
```

`AgentGrid.jsx` becomes a pure display component; the page/layout that uses it calls `getCollection('agents')` and passes the data as a prop.

---

## 6. Filter improvements

### 6a — Bathroom count always 0
`xmlParser.js:63`: `liczbalazienek: 0` is hardcoded. The Otodom format doesn't expose a direct bathroom field in most Details types, so the bathroom filter never works. Either:
- Remove the Bathrooms filter from `RealEstateFilter.jsx` until the field exists in data
- Or map it from a Details field if one exists for the property type

### 6b — Price filter tied to EUR only
The hardcoded ranges (`€2M-€5M`, `€5M-€10M`, `€10M+`) silently fail for PLN/GBP listings. Fix: make ranges currency-aware or filter on raw number values only (unit-agnostic) and display accordingly.

### 6c — Derive bedroom options from data
Same pattern as `locationOptions` in `CollectionManager`: compute `uniqueBedroomTiers` from actual offers rather than hardcoding `['3+', '5+', '8+']`.

---

## 7. reverseGeocode performance

**Current:** For every offer, iterates all 135k cities (O(n) per offer). With 20 offers that's 2.7M iterations at build time.

**Fix:** Memoize by rounded `(lat, lon)` pair (most offers in the same building share coords). A `Map` keyed on `${lat.toFixed(3)},${lon.toFixed(3)}` eliminates duplicate lookups in one line.

Also consider filtering by latitude bounding box before the haversine loop — Mediterranean properties all sit between lat 35–48, lon -10 to 30, cutting the search space roughly in half.

---

## 8. Dead code removal

| File | Issue | Action |
|------|--------|--------|
| `src/hooks/useOffers.js` | Fetches old `/offers.xml`, unused | Delete |
| `parseOffersXml()` in `xmlParser.js` | Legacy parser, no callers | Delete |
| `handleSearch()` in `RealEstateFilter.jsx` | `console.log` stub | Remove; filtering is already reactive |

---

## 9. Leaflet loading — move from CDN to npm package

**Current:** `<link>` to `unpkg.com/leaflet@1.9.4/dist/leaflet.css` in every page `<head>`. The JS is also loaded from unpkg inside `PropertyMap.jsx`.

**Fix:** Import from the installed package:
```js
// PropertyMap.jsx (already client:only="react")
import L from 'leaflet';
import 'leaflet/dist/leaflet.css';
```
Remove the `<link>` and `<script>` tags from Layout.astro. This eliminates the external CDN dependency and allows bundling/tree-shaking.

---

## 10. Font loading — reduce blocking

**Current:** Two `<link rel="stylesheet">` tags to Google Fonts (blocking render). One to Material Symbols.

**Options:**
- Add `font-display: swap` via `display=swap` param (already partially there)
- Use `@fontsource` npm packages for self-hosting (eliminates Google DNS lookup)
- At minimum: add `<link rel="preload" as="style">` before the font stylesheet links

---

## 11. Blog schema field mismatch

`content.config.ts` schema defines `thumbnail` (optional string).
`src/pages/blog/index.astro` references `entry.data.image` — this field does not exist in the schema, so blog card images never render.

**Fix:** Change the blog index page to use `entry.data.thumbnail` consistently.

---

## Summary — Priority order

| Priority | Change | Impact |
|----------|--------|--------|
| 🔴 Critical | Decide static rebuild vs SSR (§1) | Architecture |
| 🔴 Critical | Stabilise XML/photo path (§2) | Maintainability |
| 🟠 High | Add `loading="lazy"` + Astro `<Image>` thumbnails (§3) | Performance, LCP |
| 🟠 High | OG tags + sitemap + JSON-LD + robots.txt (§4) | SEO |
| 🟠 High | Fix blog image field mismatch `image` → `thumbnail` (§11) | Bug |
| 🟠 High | Fix bathroom count always 0 (§6a) | Bug |
| 🟡 Medium | Agent content collection (§5) | Maintainability |
| 🟡 Medium | Leaflet from npm instead of CDN (§9) | Reliability |
| 🟡 Medium | reverseGeocode memoization (§7) | Build speed |
| 🟡 Medium | Price filter currency-awareness (§6b) | Correctness |
| 🟢 Low | Derive bedroom options from data (§6c) | Data integrity |
| 🟢 Low | Delete dead code (§8) | Cleanliness |
| 🟢 Low | Font self-hosting (§10) | Performance |

## Verification
- `npm run build` — must complete without errors after each change
- `npm run preview` — walk through index filter, property detail, blog listing, blog post
- Check Network tab: verify lazy images don't load until scrolled into view
- Check `<head>` in source: OG tags, canonical, JSON-LD present on property pages
- Lighthouse audit on index and a property page — target LCP < 2.5s
